input.taskBrief: Tighten repair-path guidance so agents stop rerunning forge when freshness repair is the real next step.
input.currentSkill:
Forge is the workflow layer.

- active slice, implementation-ready: run wiki forge run <project> <slice> --repo <path>
- stale, conflicting, or failed state: use the repair branch below, then return to wiki forge run
input.acceptanceCriteria: Preserve command authority, make checkpoint and maintain authoritative for freshness, and tell the agent to use concrete repair commands when verify/close fails for stale-page debt.
input.repoContext: This repo uses sync:local after skill edits and requires an agent restart after syncing installed skill copies.

input.taskBrief: Clarify that generated project summary pages are derived outputs and should be refreshed through sync/maintain before manual edits.
input.currentSkill:
Wiki is for memory, verification, retrieval, and research filing.

Generated pages may exist.
input.acceptanceCriteria: Make generated pages explicit, prefer sync or maintain first, and keep the distinction between wiki maintenance and forge workflow.
input.repoContext: The repo treats project summary pages as derived knowledge surfaces and requires sync:local plus restart only when skill files themselves change.

input.taskBrief: Document the safer debugging path when project-level forge status and slice-level truth diverge.
input.currentSkill: Use forge status to inspect workflow state.
input.acceptanceCriteria: Explain that slice-scoped status is the safer debugging surface, keep forge as the workflow layer, and preserve the command authority order.
input.repoContext: Agents were confused because project-level status was noisy while slice-level status was the reliable workflow truth during debugging.

mustAvoid: wiki forge run is always the next step

input.taskBrief: Strengthen command authority around checkpoint vs maintain vs resume for stale-context disagreements.
input.currentSkill: Resume gives session context. Maintain repairs the wiki.
input.acceptanceCriteria: Make checkpoint authoritative for freshness, maintain authoritative for reconciliation, and resume context-only. Keep wiki scoped away from forge SDLC policy.
input.repoContext: The repo had repeated cases where resume still printed stale context even though checkpoint was clean and maintain had no impacted pages.

input.taskBrief: Teach the research bridge explicitly so distilled research is not mistaken for completed forge research.
input.acceptanceCriteria: Mention research file, distill, adopt, and status in the correct order. Preserve forge as workflow policy, not general research tooling.
input.repoContext: The workflow repeatedly got stuck because research existed but was never adopted into the tracked slice ledger.

mustAvoid: domain-model can start before research adoption

input.taskBrief: Clarify that historical and project-debt warnings in closeout/gate should not override active slice truth when the slice itself is ready to close.
input.currentSkill: Closeout and gate should be clean before close.
input.acceptanceCriteria: Distinguish active slice blockers from historical/project debt, and keep explicit close-path commands rather than generic reruns.
input.repoContext: Warning-heavy closeout output made agents think the slice was still blocked even when the active blockers were resolved.
